interpretation_summary: The replication executed the preregistered Wilcoxon signed-rank test on the original figure-based dataset (zhang_avgprice.csv) and reproduced the key result: the UNIFORM treatment’s group-average prices exceed the fixed price benchmark of 1.94 (n=14, p≈0.00085; median difference +0.385). However, the planned independent replication using newly collected oTree session data (round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv) was not executed within the container despite the presence of collapsed outputs (fullsample_analysis.csv). Those outputs indicate a median group-average price of 1.969 (slightly above 1.94), but no test statistic or p-value is available from the executed run. Overall, the replication partially followed the preregistration: it confirmed the original figure-based finding but did not complete the new-sample replication test.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Partial alignment: The preregistration specified a two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test against 1.94 and outlined two analyses: (1) reproduce the original figure-based result on zhang_avgprice.csv and (2) perform the same test on newly collected UNIFORM groups from round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv after filtering out dropout/bankrupt groups. The execution ran the original-figure reproduction (analysis_plan__py.py) as planned, but did not run the new-sample analysis entrypoint (replication_analysis__py.py). Statistical method (two-sided Wilcoxon) matched the preregistration for the executed portion.

deviations.issue_description: Entrypoint deviation: Executed only analysis_plan__py.py (original figure-based test) instead of also executing replication_analysis__py.py (new-sample replication).
deviations.impact: Moderate: The independent replication on the new dataset was not assessed, preventing a full evaluation of the preregistered focal replication on new data.

deviations.issue_description: Use of original aggregated data (zhang_avgprice.csv) rather than newly collapsed outputs for the main replication conclusion.
deviations.impact: Low-to-Moderate: Reproduces the original result but does not speak to generalizability; leaves uncertainty about the effect in the new sessions.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: The UNIFORM treatment’s group-average market price differs from (expected greater than) 1.94, tested via a two-sided Wilcoxon signed-rank test.
results_comparison.original_results: Wilcoxon signed-rank on UNIFORM group averages vs 1.94: n=14, p=0.001; average UNIFORM price ≈ 2.34; only 2 of 14 groups had averages below 1.94.
results_comparison.replication_results: Executed on original figure-based data (zhang_avgprice.csv): n=14, Wilcoxon statistic=4.0, p=0.000854, median difference (avgprice−1.94)=+0.3850. Additional outputs show for the new combined sample (fullsample_analysis.csv, N=40 groups) a median group-average price of 1.969, but no executed Wilcoxon test or p-value is available to determine significance.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Partial: The reproduction of the original figure-based test supports the focal claim (p<0.01, correct direction). The planned independent replication on new data was not executed; thus, preregistered comparison criteria for the new sample cannot be assessed.

replication_report: Partial replication: The original figure-based test replicates strongly (Wilcoxon p≈0.00085; median +0.385 above 1.94). The independent new-sample replication was not executed, though collapsed outputs suggest a median slightly above 1.94; statistical significance remains unverified.

failure_handling.suggestions: Execute replication_data/replication_analysis__py.py as preregistered to compute Wilcoxon results on the new oTree datasets (round1_raw.csv and round2_raw.csv), applying the specified exclusions (dropout/bankrupt). Report n, statistic, p-value, and median difference.

Emit and archive the full set of outputs for the new-sample test (e.g., round-level and group-level summaries, filtered fullsample_analysis.csv, and test outputs). If both unfiltered and filtered results are produced, label them clearly and include both two-sided and one-sided (greater) p-values, given directional expectations.

notes: The new-sample collapsed output indicates several group averages below 1.94 and at least one zero average, underscoring the importance of applying the preregistered exclusion rules (dropout/bankrupt) before the Wilcoxon test. Differences in platform (oTree) and subject pools may affect variance and effect size; recommend sensitivity checks by session and by filtering status. The replication appears robust for the original figure-based result, but conclusions for the new sample await the unexecuted analysis.
